data.title: AI inference startup Baseten confirms $300M in new funding at $5B valuation
data.description:
Baseten, a startup focused on providing inference for artificial intelligence applications, said on Friday that it has raised $300M in a Series E funding round, confirming previous reports.

The new funding round, which includes Nvidia (NVDA), Battery Ventures, Blackbird Ventures, and others as investors, values the AI startup at $5B. 

“The last few years have been exhilarating: last year alone inference volume grew 100x,” Baseten wrote on its website. “We’ve had the privilege of partnering with some of the fastest-growing, highest-quality companies in the space—Abridge, Clay, Cursor, OpenEvidence, Mercor, Notion, and others—and a front-row seat to the real value they’re delivering to users. Looking ahead, demand for inference will only accelerate. Thinking and reasoning models require orders of magnitude more compute, and AI adoption across consumers and enterprises continues to compound. As AI becomes embedded in every product and workflow, inference isn’t just growing—it’s becoming one of the largest markets ever created. We’re building the software that powers it.”

The $300M Series E funding round is the company's third capital raise in 12 months.

Nebius (NBIS) recently partnered with Baseten to offer AI companies and enterprises text-to-video inferencing across all its cloud regions in the U.S., Finland, and France.

data.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon's annual pay jumps 21% to $47M
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) Chairman and CEO David Solomon's total 2025 compensation rose to $47M from  $39M a year earlier, a 21% Y/Y jump, the investment bank disclosed on Friday.

He received a $2M base salary along with a $45M bonus in the form of stock, cash and carried interest, according to a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filing.

In making its determination, the compensation committee considered a slew of positive developments, from significant shareholder value (+57% total shareholder return, +33% in quarterly dividend, and +6.2% book value per share) to strong financial performance (+27% in diluted EPS and a 230-basis-point improvement in return on average common equity).

By comparison, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon earned $43M in 2025, up 10.2% Y/Y.

data.title: Intel 10-K highlights external foundry revenue, potential upside for AMD: Wells Fargo
data.description:
After Intel (INTC) filed its annual 10-K report with its fourth-quarter results, some notable findings include the semiconductor giant's external foundry revenue and possible benefits for AMD (AMD), Wells Fargo said.

External foundry revenue for the full-year was $307M, including $222M in the fourth-quarter, up sharply from the $159M in 2024, Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers wrote in a note to clients. However, the de-consolidation of Altera, which Intel partially sold to private equity firm Silver Lake in September 2025, contributed to fourth-quarter external revenue, Rakers added.

Intel generated $4.51B in foundry-related revenue for the period ending Dec. 27, out of a total $13.67B.

Rakers has an Equal-Weight rating and $45 price target on Intel.

Additionally, Rakers said the 10-K disclosed that 2025 server CPU volume grew 9% year-over-year. However, the average selling price fell 4%, due in part to pricing actions taken in the first-half of the year and a higher mix of “lower core count products,” Rakers added. As such, he believes that fourth-quarter shipments of server CPUs grew 8% year-over-year, down from 13% growth in the second-quarter and flat in the third quarter.

“While we have not yet seen preliminary 4Q25 server ship estimates, we note that Micron's (MU) outlook that CY25 server ship would increase ~15% y/y (reflecting a strong acceleration [through] 2HCY25; Gartner estimates 1Q25-3Q25 was +1% y/y) would imply 4Q25 server shipments up 15%-17% y/y,” Rakers said. “Our estimate of Intel's Xeon CPU ship at +8% y/y would leave us incrementally positive on AMD's server ship results for 4Q25.”

Intel shares tumbled more than 17% on Friday, while AMD shares rose more than 2%.

data.title: Life360 rallies more than 20% on preliminary results
data.description:
Life360, Inc. (LIF) jumped more than 20% on Friday following the company's announcement that it will report a strong operational performance for Q4 2025. The company revealed preliminary results that included Q4 Monthly Active Users of 95.8M, with the highest Q4 MAU additions in the firm's history.

The company added that full-year 2025 net additions reached 16.2 million, representing 20% growth YoY. For Q4, U.S. MAU rose 16% from last year, while international growth hit 26%.

The stock was higher by $12.25 in late Friday trading, climbing to $67.06. This represented a rally of more than 22%.

LIF had been drifting lower since October, coming off of a 52-week high of $112.54. The stock currently has a Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of 3.07, representing a Hold, with an A grade for Growth but a D- for valuation.

data.title: Booz Allen narrows revenue guidance to $11.3B-$11.4B while advancing outcome-based contracts and AI strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Booz Allen Hamilton Holding Corporation (BAH) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Horacio Rozanski, Chair, CEO & President, began by acknowledging the CFO transition, stating, "Matt Calderone will be leaving on February 1 and today is his final Booz Allen earnings call." He noted Kristine Martin Anderson will step in as Interim CFO. Rozanski emphasized progress on three strategic priorities: reducing costs, accelerating the shift to outcome-based contracting and product sales, and focusing investments in growth areas like cyber, national security, partnerships, and AI. He said, "Our third quarter results demonstrate we're making strong progress against those priorities. Our results are in line with the revised fiscal year guidance we shared in October, and we are narrowing the ranges at the top and bottom lines."
  • Rozanski highlighted the launch of Vellox Reverser, an AI-native malware reverse engineering product, and announced a new partnership with Andreessen Horowitz, with Booz Allen committing "to deploy up to $400 million in a16z's late-stage venture fund over the life of the fund."
  • Matthew Calderone, Executive VP & CFO, stated, "our third quarter results are in line with the revised fiscal year guidance we issued in October, even with the protracted shutdown. Revenue ex billables, where most of our profitability is generated, tracked in line with expectations. And both adjusted EBITDA and ADEPS were stronger than anticipated."
  • Calderone noted that the cost reduction program will result in "dropping our run rate spend by approximately $150 million. The full impact of these cost actions on profitability will be felt next fiscal year."

Outlook

  • Kristine Anderson, Executive VP & COO, provided updated fiscal year 2026 guidance: "We now expect to deliver revenue between $11.3 billion and $11.4 billion, given some of the impacts from the prolonged government shutdown. We expect an adjusted EBITDA dollar range of between $1.195 billion and $1.215 billion. We are raising our ADEPS guidance to a range of $5.95 to $6.15 per share. Lastly, we expect to generate free cash flow between $825 million and $900 million."
  • Anderson commented on the pipeline, saying, "Demand for our national security technology and expertise remains robust. At the same time, our Civil business is reset and demand is accelerating."

Financial Results

  • Calderone reported, "gross revenue totaled $2.6 billion, representing a roughly 10% decline versus the prior year period and a 7% decline on a revenue ex-billable basis."
  • National security portfolio declined about 1% year-over-year in the quarter, but adjusting for the shutdown, it grew about 4% year-over-year.
  • Civil business declined about 28% year-over-year.
  • Net bookings for the third quarter totaled $888 million, with a quarterly book-to-bill ratio of 0.3x and a record year-end backlog of over $38 billion, up about 2% over the prior year.
  • Booz Allen ended the quarter with $882 million of cash on hand and net debt of $3.1 billion. Free cash flow for the quarter was $248 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the third quarter was $285 million, translating to an adjusted EBITDA margin of 10.9%. Net income was $200 million. Adjusted diluted earnings per share increased about 14% year-over-year to $1.77 per share.

Q&A

  • Colin Canfield, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about defense and intelligence growth versus civil, and the multiyear outlook for the civil business. Horacio Rozanski responded that "the market does feel like it's at an inflection point" and expressed optimism about AI and cyber growth. Kristine Anderson added that modernization in civil "has moved from a focus on cloud to a focus on readiness of data platforms that are critical for AI."
  • Gautam Khanna, TD Cowen: Sought details on the cost reduction plan. Calderone replied, "The actions are done. We'll see a little bit of the impact in Q4, but this really is about setting us up for next year."
  • Sheila Kahyaoglu, Jefferies: Queried about civil's return to growth and shifts in government sales strategy. Anderson said, "the civil business overall had -- this has been a year of reset," and Rozanski emphasized "we continue to drive our own work towards outcome-based" contracts.
  • Scott Mikus, Melius Research: Asked if organic revenue growth will outpace headcount. Calderone confirmed, "we're also getting more leverage out of technology as even the things Horacio described, you were leading with solutions that are more tech forward."
  • John Godyn, Citi: Inquired on defense budget outlook and strategic positioning. Rozanski stated, "we have been preparing all along to support those priorities," referencing investments in cyber and AI.
  • Seth Seifman, JPMorgan: Asked about funded backlog and competition. Anderson noted, "we are seeing really positive signs...strong funding in December, so far in January, very strong, an improved demand environment, pipeline's up."
  • Tobey Sommer, Truist: Asked about capital deployment and civil as a source of fiscal restraint. Rozanski said, "The balance sheet is strong and it's a strategic asset, and we continue to look for, call it, smaller but impactful M&A."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, with questions focusing on outlook for civil, cost reductions, and competitive positioning. Several analysts expressed appreciation for outgoing CFO Matt Calderone.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently referencing "strong execution" and "progress against priorities." Rozanski stated, "our performance this quarter and our progress against our 3 priorities gives me confidence that we are on track operationally and strategically."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone has shifted from disappointment and caution to greater confidence and emphasis on execution and positioning for future growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance has tightened, with the revenue range now $11.3B-$11.4B compared to $11.3B-$11.5B previously. ADEPS guidance has increased from $5.45-$5.65 to $5.95-$6.15 per share.
  • Cost reduction actions, previously announced, have now been executed, with the full impact expected next fiscal year.
  • While national security remains the growth engine, civil business decline accelerated from 22% year-over-year in Q2 to 28% in Q3, but management now notes "green shoots" and a growing pipeline in civil.
  • Analyst focus has shifted from concerns about further guidance cuts to questions on timing of civil recovery, cost takeout realization, and leveraging technology for growth.
  • Management confidence has improved, highlighting progress on outcome-based contracting, cost actions, and robust technology partnerships.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the prolonged government shutdown as causing revenue and profit impacts, as well as delays in funding and awards.
  • Calderone stated, "the shutdown pushed some procurements and funding actions to the right," estimating a cumulative impact of about $50 million on revenue and $20 million on profit for the full fiscal year.
  • Competition from new commercial entrants and evolving tech partnerships were noted, but management believes their ecosystem provides an edge.
  • Ongoing uncertainty in the funding environment and the need for agility were emphasized as areas requiring continued focus.

Final Takeaway

Leadership highlighted strong execution against strategic priorities, the successful launch of new AI cybersecurity products, and expanded partnerships as positioning Booz Allen for a return to growth. Despite near-term pressures from the government shutdown and civil sector declines, management expressed confidence that cost reductions, a robust national security pipeline, and increasing demand for outcome-based solutions and advanced technology will drive future financial improvement and margin expansion.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Microsoft earnings, Boeing buzz, FOMC meeting, and fintech IPOs
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - January 26

  • Volatility watch - AppLovin (APP) and Salesforce (CRM) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include ImmunityBio (IBRX), Corvus Pharmaceuticals (CRVS), and Masonglory (MSGY). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Abbott (ABT), Netflix (NFLX), and Diginex (DGNX).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nucor (NUE), Brown & Brown (BRO), and Crane Company (CR).
  • IPO watch - Brazilian fintech upstart Picpay (PICS) and insurance tech company Ethos Technologies (LIFE) are expected to price their IPOs and begin to trade. The IPO lockup period expires for certain blocks of shares of Ambiq Micro (AMBQ) and Shoulder Innovations (SI).
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Clorox (CLX), Paychex (PAYX), Costco (COST), and Morgan Stanley (MS).
  • All day - Hecla Mining (HL) will hold an Investor Day event in New York City. Presentations will be made by the senior management covering strategy, finance, operations, exploration, sustainability, and innovation.

 

Tuesday - January 27

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Boeing (BA), RTX (RTX), Texas Instruments (TXN), Union Pacific (UNP), Northrop Grumman (NOC), UPS (UPS), General Motors (GM), and Kimberly-Clark (KMB). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Seagate Technology (STX) and JetBlue (JBLU) after they report.
  • All day - India Energy Week will kick off in Goa. The event is India’s flagship oil, gas, and broader energy exhibition and conference.
  • All day - The all-stock merger between PotlatchDeltic (PCH) and Rayonier (RYN) will go to a shareholder vote. The New Gold (NGD)-Coeur Mining (CDE) and SM Energy (SM)-Civitas Resources (CIVI) deals also go to a vote.
  • All day - Visa (V) will hold its annual meeting. Shareholders will vote on typical board and corporate governance matters, though management may comment on strategy themes.
  • 8:30 a.m. The S&P/Case-Shiller House Price Index will be released.
  • 8:30 a.m. The New Home Sales report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
  • 10:30 a.m. Boeing (BA) will hold its earnings call. Updates on 737 MAX 8/9 output, the planned activation of the Everett North Line, and the timeline for 737-10 certification will be closely watched. Astronics (ATRO) derives 12% of its revenue from Boeing and trades in tandem with it following earnings 75% of the time.

 

Wednesday - January 28

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), Tesla (TSLA), ASML (ASML), IBM (IBM), Starbucks (SBUX), General Dynamics (GD), and Las Vegas Sands (LVS). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Brinker International (EAT) and LendingClub (LC) after they report.
  • All day - The Nevada Gaming Control Board is expected to release its gaming revenue report for December.
  • 8:30 a.m. The December Durable Goods order report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will announce its interest rate decision.
  • 2:30 p.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will hold a press conference.
  • 5:30 p.m. Microsoft (MSFT) will hold its earnings conference call. Key topics will include AI and Azure growth and margin forecasts, Copilot monetization trends, and AI capex spending. Options trading is pricing in a +5% move following the earnings release, which would be a bigger post-earnings swing than normal.

 

Thursday - January 29

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Apple (AAPL), Visa (V), Mastercard (MA), Caterpillar (CAT), Honeywell (HON), Lockheed Martin (LMT), Comcast (CMCSA), Altria (MO), and Deutsche Bank (DB). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Western Digital (WDC) and Olin (OLN) after they report.
  • All day - Starbucks (SBUX) will hold a key Investor Day event.
  • M&A watch - Shareholders with Kenvue (KVUE) and Kimberly-Clark (KMB) vote on the proposed $49B merger between the companies. Shareholders with REV Group (REVG) and Terex (TEX) will vote on their planned combination.

 

Friday - January 30

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Exxon Mobil (XOM), Chevron (CVX), American Express (AXP), Verizon (VZ), Colgate-Palmolive (CL), and SoFi Technologies (SOFI). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for Charter Communications (CHTR) after it reports.
  • M&A watch - The Sealed Air (SEE) sale to CD&R could be ruled on by the Competition Canada and European Commission. Sealed Air (SEE) traded less than 1% from the deal price at the time of publication.
  • All day - Church & Dwight (CHD) will hold its Analyst Day event.
  • 8:30 a.m. The December Producer Price Index report will be released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.
  • 10:00 a.m. It is the government funding deadline.

data.title: Tesla removes autopilot suite on new Model 3 and Y vehicles
data.description:
• Tesla (TSLA) has discontinued the Autopilot suite for all new Model 3 and Model Y vehicles sold in the U.S. and Canada, equipping new cars with only Traffic Aware Cruise Control ((TACC)), in an effort to increase subscriptions for its full self-driving software.
  • The Autopilot suite includes both TACC and Autosteer.
  • Last week, Tesla (TSLA) said it would stop charging an $8,000 one-time fee for full self-driving software after February 14 and instead charge $99 per month for access to the software.

data.title: Southern Copper cut to Sell equivalent, Vale tapped as top metals pick at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
Southern Copper (SCCO) +4.1% in Friday's trading as copper futures rallied above $13.1K/ton on the London Metal Exchange, part of a broad-based surge in metal markets aided by a weaker dollar that has declined by the most in any week since June.

The Greenland crisis in particular added to the sense of unpredictability in U.S. policymaking, weakening the dollar and adding momentum to safer sectors such as copper, which has been rising since mid-2025 due to major mine disruptions, strong demand from electrification, and a surge in shipments to the U.S. ahead of possible tariffs.

Southern Copper (SCCO) trades higher despite a downgrade at J.P. Morgan to Underweight from Neutral with a $117.50 price target, citing the stock's recent rally and the expectation for lower copper prices in the near term.

JPM commodities analyst Greg Shearer highlighted several red flags indicating copper is exposed to a near-term correction after such a rapid move on limited fundamental change; he expects the upcoming Chinese New Year to further dampen market activity, increasing the probability of a price pullback toward the $12K/mt range in the coming months, which could help restore Chinese demand.

Despite its rally, Vale (VALE) is JPM's top pick for investors seeking metals opportunities, believing iron ore has contrarian price action compared to copper and for iron ore prices to keep up good momentum in Q1; the bank sees Vale trading at 5x EV/EBITDA 2026 vs. peers at 5.9x.

J.P. Morgan's Shearer said his tactical caution does not alter his constructive longer-term view on copper, given favorable supply-demand dynamics.

ETFs: (COPX), (CPER), (JJCTF)

data.title: Live Nation gains after court hearing on DOJ's antitrust lawsuit ends
data.description:
Live Nation (LYV) rose 5.8% after a court hearing over the company's attempt to have the Dept. of Justice's antitrust lawsuit thrown out ended. 

The court hearing in Manhattan, before Judge Arun Subramanian, ended on Friday without a ruling from the judge, a court observer told Seeking Alpha. The judge didn't indicate when he would make a ruling. 

The concert and ticketing company was trying to get a summary judgement that would end the government's case or decide the case without need for a jury trial.  

In 2024, the DOJ and more than two dozen states filed an antitrust suit against Ticketmaster parent Live Nation (LYV), looking to break up the company. The lawsuit alleged that an illegal monopoly on live events pushes prices higher for fans.

data.title: Cohen & Steers signals 8% REIT earnings growth for 2026 amid strategic distribution expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cohen & Steers (CNS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Joseph Harvey, CEO, highlighted that "we ended 2025 with good momentum across key business metrics. We saw positive flows into nearly all vehicles. Fee rates were stable. Our institutional pipeline continued to strengthen and we are making progress on distribution initiatives." Harvey noted net inflows of $1.28 billion for the quarter and $1.5 billion for the year, driven by advisory, U.S. REITs, global listed infrastructure, and a $513 million inflow from a rights offering for the infrastructure closed-end fund. He also emphasized initiatives around expanding global distribution, particularly the promotion of Dan Noonan to Head of Global Distribution, and a focus on increasing coverage in the RIA channel, sub-advisory, and international institutional presence.
  • Michael Donohue, Interim CFO, stated "reported earnings of $0.81 per share, which equaled EPS reported in the prior quarter. Earnings for the full year 2025 was $3.09 per share compared to $2.93 in 2024." Donohue added, "Revenue for Q4 increased 2% sequentially to $143.8 million. Revenues for the full year increased 6.9% versus the prior year to $554 million." He pointed out that net inflows made Q4 the fifth out of six trailing quarters with inflows and operating income rose 3% to $52.4 million during the quarter. Liquidity at year-end totaled $403 million.
  • John Cheigh, President & Chief Investment Officer, highlighted long-term outperformance, stating "on a 1-year basis, 95% of our AUM has outperformed its benchmark, while our 3-, 5- and 10-year outperformance rates are all above 95%." Cheigh also noted, "our nontraded REIT, CNS REIT, has enjoyed a 10.3% annualized return since its January 2024 inception, which is more than double the return of the median equity nontraded REIT."

Outlook

  • Donohue indicated, "we would expect our compensation ratio to remain at 40%. We continue to maintain a disciplined approach to managing talent by balancing our business needs and strategic priorities with revenue growth." He further noted, "we expect annual G&A growth in 2026 to moderate from 2025 and are projecting it to be in the mid-single-digit percentage range."
  • Regarding tax guidance, Donohue said, "we expect our effective tax rate to be 25.4% on an as-adjusted basis."
  • Cheigh forecasted, "we expect the combination of lower supply and accelerating economic growth and thus demand to result in accelerating REIT earnings above trend to roughly 8% in 2026 and 2027."

Financial Results

  • Donohue reported, "Revenue for Q4 increased 2% sequentially to $143.8 million," and "operating income increased 3% to $52.4 million during the quarter." He noted, "ending AUM in Q4 was $90.5 billion, which was down slightly from the end of Q3. However, as noted earlier, we experienced higher average AUM during Q4 as compared to the prior quarter."
  • Net inflows during Q4 were $1.2 billion, primarily related to advisory and closed-end funds, offset by market depreciation and distributions. The compensation ratio decreased to 39% for the quarter.
  • Liquidity at year-end totaled $403 million, representing a $39 million increase versus the prior quarter end.

Q&A

  • John Dunn, Evercore ISI: "I wanted to ask a little more on the private real estate. It seems like we're seeing signs of some improving demand for that asset class. First of all, are you seeing that? And then also, do you think it can be a more significant contributor in 2026?" CEO Harvey responded, "I think we're early in the process of investor interest coming back to private real estate, but we're seeing some good early phase signs... We're gaining critical mass and we're starting to broaden the number of platforms that we're on. So we're ready in case that transition does follow through."
  • Dunn: "Do you think active ETFs can scale more quickly for you guys?" Harvey answered, "Absolutely... there are core strategies. They're just delivered in a different vehicle and the market has voted that active ETFs are the way to go for the future... we've got our milestones. We want to get the vehicles as we launch them to scale so that allocators know they can come in and have efficient trading spreads on them."
  • Rodrigo Ferreira, BofA Securities: "Can you talk a little bit about the recent progress in the institutional channel?" Harvey said, "we've now had a very strong pipeline for 2 quarters in a row. This quarter, I'd say that, that pipeline has broadened a little bit. It's deepened a little bit with the number of mandates, the location of the domicile of the allocator and the range of strategies."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a neutral-to-positive tone, with questions focusing on scaling opportunities for private real estate and ETFs, and institutional channel progress, seeking clarity on potential growth areas.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, reiterating strong performance and future potential. Cheigh remarked, "we continue to meet our objective of providing long-term alpha for our clients." Harvey was optimistic about distribution initiatives and product momentum.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management sustained a similar constructive tone, with management slightly more bullish regarding product scalability and institutional pipeline depth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 guidance language maintained stability, with compensation and tax ratios aligned to previous projections but with a more explicit projection for G&A growth moderation.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward realizing returns on recent investments, particularly in active ETFs, private real estate, and expansion in global distribution.
  • Analysts in both quarters centered questions on product performance, distribution, and institutional flows, but Q4 saw more discussion about rapid scaling of ETFs and early signs of private real estate demand.
  • Management's confidence increased around pipeline depth, scalability, and readiness for new product adoption, compared to a more cautious outlook in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted increased G&A expenses due to travel, business development, and talent acquisition costs.
  • Harvey acknowledged "known terminations" in client accounts but stated these have transitioned to a more typical low level of activity.
  • Private credit market shifts and competition remain a factor affecting flows, particularly in preferred strategies.

Final Takeaway

Cohen & Steers concluded 2025 with strong momentum across key products and channels, supported by net inflows, stable fee rates, and a deepening institutional pipeline. Management expressed optimism for continued growth in 2026, particularly in REIT earnings, private real estate, and scalable ETF strategies, with targeted investments in global distribution and a disciplined approach to costs and talent. The company believes it is well positioned to capitalize on shifting asset allocations and market leadership, aiming for accelerated growth and enhanced returns on recent strategic initiatives.
